root_cause: The web service was configured with MAX_RETRIES=4 and DEP_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 while the downstream pricing service was degraded, taking about 2 seconds and failing every request. Under load concurrency 16 with only 8 web workers, each order request held a worker through repeated slow dependency attempts for up to roughly 25 seconds, starving the worker pool so health checks timed out even though the container was running and CPU was near idle.
actions_taken: Confirmed container status and low CPU with docker ps/stats, inspected web and pricing logs and environment, reproduced the slow /order behavior, and reviewed the compose configuration and source comments. Applied the narrow mitigation by setting RETRY_STORM_DISABLED=1 for only the web service in docker-compose.yaml, then ran docker compose up -d web to recreate only web. Verified the new web log showed bounded mode with a 400ms dependency timeout, confirmed the environment included RETRY_STORM_DISABLED=1, and repeatedly verified /healthz returned HTTP 200 in a few milliseconds under ongoing load while /order failed fast instead of exhausting workers.
postmortem: This was a retry storm caused by an unhealthy interaction between a persistently degraded dependency and aggressive caller retry settings. No single person caused the incident; the system lacked protective retry limits, tight timeouts, and load-shedding defaults for a known slow downstream failure mode. Follow-ups should make bounded retries and short per-attempt timeouts the default, add alerts for worker-pool saturation and dependency latency/error rates, test degraded-dependency scenarios under load, and consider graceful order degradation or queueing behavior if pricing is unavailable.
